option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
import crypto from 'crypto';
/** * Generates a cryptographically secure random token. * * Strategy for email verification tokens: * - 32 random bytes → 64-character hex string. * - Stored as plain text in app.email_verification_tokens.token (NVARCHAR 255). * - The token itself is random enough (256 bits of entropy) that it does not * need to be hashed — unlike refresh tokens which are JWTs and need * hash-based revocation checks. * - Expires after EMAIL_VERIFICATION_TOKEN_EXPIRES_HOURS hours (default 24). * - Marked used_at when consumed; expired or used tokens are rejected. * * @param bytes Number of random bytes (default 32 → 64-char hex output) */ export function generateSecureToken(bytes = 32): string { return crypto.randomBytes(bytes).toString('hex'); }

option.name: updatedContent
updatedContent
